rationale: Review 2's findings (private general helpers; rfl/specialized @[simp] lemmas like includeRight_apply, map_tmul, map_to*, antipode_tmul) mis-evaluate reuse: the diff intentionally scopes internal proofs private and wraps only the new baseChange API (includeRight, map, etc.) in the TauCeti namespace for usability, with all other reuse of Mathlib's TensorProduct/Bialgebra.* already direct+attributed and nothing duplicated. Acting on Review 2 forces needless refactors/deletions whose net effect on the merged PR is zero or worse for downstream users, while Review 1's approve correctly leaves the novel content untouched.
